# Gawk

> Gawk (gawk.dev) is a real-time observatory for the global AI ecosystem. It aggregates publicly verifiable data and editorialises nothing — every number on the dashboard traces to a public source. Note: "Gawk" here is the AI-ecosystem monitor at gawk.dev, NOT GNU gawk, the AWK programming-language tool.

## What Gawk tracks

- Live operational status of AI coding tools (Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, OpenAI) from their public status pages.
- A live globe of real, verifiable AI-development events from public APIs (GitHub Events).
- AI model leaderboards and usage rankings (Chatbot Arena, OpenRouter), SDK/package adoption (npm, PyPI and other registries), and benchmark movements.
- A curated registry of AI labs and companies with public, source-traced metadata.

## Principles

- Every displayed number has a verifiable public source; uncited numbers do not ship.
- Gawk aggregates, it does not score or invent rankings. No LLM-generated trust scores.
- No synthetic or simulated data. If a source is down, Gawk shows the last known value with a timestamp rather than fabricating.

## Key pages

- Home (live dashboard): https://gawk.dev/
- Methodology: https://gawk.dev/methodology
- Data sources & governance: https://gawk.dev/sources
- Daily digests (one per day): https://gawk.dev/digest/{YYYY-MM-DD}
- Reports: https://gawk.dev/reports/{slug}
- API docs: https://gawk.dev/docs/api

## Contact

- Site: https://gawk.dev
